old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-yvk7","title":"LLSec byte not signed - mode flags can be modified without detection","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:174-206

The signable data explicitly excludes the LLSec byte:

    '''NOT signed:
    - LLSec byte: Contains mode flags, not security-relevant content'''
    
    return (
        bytes([epoch])
        + seqnum.to_bytes(2, 'big')
        + dst_addr
        + payload
    )

But LLSec contains security-relevant flags:
- bit 5: signature_present - attacker could flip to 0, frame treated as unsigned
- bit 6: encrypted - attacker could flip, causing decryption failure or bypass
- bits 0-1: addr_mode - changing this changes dst_addr interpretation

An attacker who can modify frames in transit could:
1. Change signature_present from 1 to 0
2. Frame fails policy check 'unsigned frame rejected' but appears as valid unsigned frame
3. Or change addr_mode, causing dst_addr to be misinterpreted

The LLSec byte should be included in signed data since it affects security interpretation.

Note: MIC covers LLSec (per comment at line 69-70), but MIC verification is stubbed out.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:02.121913Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:02.121951Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-yvk7","title":"LLSec byte not signed - mode flags can be modified without detection","description":"File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:174-206

The signable data explicitly excludes the LLSec byte:

    '''NOT signed:
    - LLSec byte: Contains mode flags, not security-relevant content'''
    
    return (
        bytes([epoch])
        + seqnum.to_bytes(2, 'big')
        + dst_addr
        + payload
    )

But LLSec contains security-relevant flags:
- bit 5: signature_present - attacker could flip to 0, frame treated as unsigned
- bit 6: encrypted - attacker could flip, causing decryption failure or bypass
- bits 0-1: addr_mode - changing this changes dst_addr interpretation

An attacker who can modify frames in transit could:
1. Change signature_present from 1 to 0
2. Frame fails policy check 'unsigned frame rejected' but appears as valid unsigned frame
3. Or change addr_mode, causing dst_addr to be misinterpreted

The LLSec byte should be included in signed data since it affects security interpretation.

Note: MIC covers LLSec (per comment at line 69-70), but MIC verification is stubbed out.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:02.121913Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:20:23.554624151Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:20:23.554624151Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:25:23.554624151Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:20:23.554624151Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Commit: 7bb6ad2c0\n_build_signable_data now includes llsec byte (bytes([length, llsec, epoch])...). LLSec flags covered by signature. MIC also covers it. Attacker modification now detectable. P2 fixed."}
LLSec now included in signed data; commit 7bb6ad2c0
